enriched_points.expanded_content: Self-assembly enables rapid and spontaneous organization of components, resulting in structured nanocomposites that are uniformly integrated within a highly ordered environment.
The assembly process relies on non-covalent interactions, such as hydrophobic forces and electrostatic attractions, which dictate the arrangement and stability of the nanocomposite structures.

An example is the assembly of silica nanoparticles with polymers, creating multifunctional nanocomposites that exhibit enhanced mechanical, thermal, and electrical properties for diverse applications.
